name: TradingView MCP Server
what_it_does: An MCP server that connects AI assistants to TradingView market data, enabling real-time screening for top gainers/losers, technical indicator analysis (RSI, MACD, Bollinger Bands), and pattern recognition across crypto and stock exchanges.
use_cases: Natural language queries for market screening ('Show top 10 crypto gainers on Binance in 15 minutes')
AI-assisted technical analysis with Bollinger Band squeeze detection and candlestick pattern recognition
Multi-exchange monitoring spanning KuCoin, Binance, Bybit, NASDAQ, NYSE, and BIST
Automated indicator analysis integrated into AI trading research workflows
not_for: Automated trade execution (this is analysis-only, not a broker integration)
Real-time tick data or order book depth analysis
Financial advice or regulated investment services
best_when: You want an AI assistant to help with market research and technical analysis using TradingView data without switching between tools.
avoid_when: You need actual trade execution, real-time streaming data, or sub-minute latency for trading decisions.

auth.notes: No authentication required. Uses TradingView public data APIs. No TradingView account needed.

pricing.notes: MIT licensed. TradingView rate limits on public APIs apply. No direct cost for the MCP server itself.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Community project; error handling for rate limits and data unavailability mentioned but not well specified.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Market data reads are idempotent (same query returns same snapshot at same point in time).
known_agent_gotchas: TradingView public API rate limiting can cause intermittent failures - retry logic not documented
Data availability varies by exchange and timeframe - some combinations may return empty results
Not a regulated financial data provider - not suitable for compliance-sensitive trading applications
Community project with no guaranteed maintenance or SLA
TradingView may change or restrict their public APIs without notice
